title: tephrosia adunca var. genuina
intro: Tephrosia adunca var. genuina, also known as the narrow-leaved spider flower, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family. It is primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions of Australia and Asia and has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and roots are used to make herbal preparations, which have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, providing health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to treat various ailments, including coughs, colds, and skin conditions, with herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures being commonly used.
